question_structure.context: Loading and unloading are essential components of the logistics process. A company has designed belt conveyors as shown in Figures A and B. The speed of the conveyor is adjustable, allowing different speeds to be set for different types of goods. The length of the conveyor is also adjustable, enabling it to extend directly into trailers for operation. Additionally, the height of the conveyor can be adjusted, making it convenient for workers to directly handle goods located at higher positions in the cargo compartment.
question_structure.sub_question_1: If cargo $A$ (which can be considered as a particle) is at the same height as the conveyor, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure C. It is known that the length of the conveyor is $L_{1}=6\,\mathrm{m}$, and the conveyor moves clockwise at a speed of $v_{0}=2\,\mathrm{m/s}$. The kinetic friction coefficient between cargo $A$ and the conveyor is $\mu_{1}=0.2$. Cargo $A$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor. Find the acceleration of cargo $A$ when it is just placed on the conveyor and the time it takes to travel from the left end to the right end. (Take $g=10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.)
question_structure.sub_question_2: If the cargo $B$ (which can be considered as a point mass) is at a higher position, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure D. It is known that the length of the conveyor becomes $L_{2}=12.05\mathrm{m}$, the inclination angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$, the dynamic friction coefficient between cargo $B$ and the conveyor $\mu_{2}=0.5$, and cargo $B$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor, which still rotates clockwise. When the speed of cargo $B$ at the right end exceeds $7\mathrm{m/s}$, the cargo will be damaged due to collision. To ensure the safety of the cargo, what condition should the speed $v_{0}$ of the conveyor satisfy? ($\sin37^{\circ}=0.6$, $\cos37^{\circ}=0.8$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)

sub_question_1.step_1:
Taking object $A$ as the subject of study, a force analysis is conducted. According to Newton's second law, we have  
$N_{1}=m g$  
$f_{1}=m a_{1}$  
$f_{1}=\mu N_{1}$  
Substituting the data yields  
$a_{1}=2\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$
sub_question_1.step_2: When cargo $A$ reaches the same speed as the conveyor, according to the kinematic formula $v_{0}^{2}=2a_{1}s_{1}$, we can obtain: $s_{1}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{2a_{1}}=1\mathrm{m}$
sub_question_1.step_3: According to the kinematic formula $v_{0}=a_{1}t_{1}$, we can obtain $t_{1}=1\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_1.step_4: After the cargo $A$ moves at a constant velocity, $L_{1}-s_{1}=v_{0}t_{2}$, we can obtain: $t_{2}=2.5\mathrm{s}$
sub_question_1.step_5: The total time for the movement of cargo $A$ is $t=3.5\mathrm{s}$.

sub_question_2.step_6:
Taking object $B$ as the subject of study, performing a force analysis, and according to Newton's second law, we obtain
$N_{2}=m g\cos\theta$
$m g\sin\theta+f_{2}=m a_{2}$
$f_{2}=\mu_{2}N_{2}$
sub_question_2.step_7: Bringing in the data, we obtain $a_{2}=10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
sub_question_2.step_8: When cargo $B$ reaches the same speed as the conveyor, $v_{0}^{2}=2a_{2}s_{2}$ can be obtained, leading to $s_{2}=\frac{v_{0}^{2}}{2a_{2}}{=}2\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_2.step_9: After the cargo $B$ continues to accelerate, the force analysis according to Newton's second law yields $m g\sin\theta - f_{2} = m a_{3}$. Substituting the values gives $a_{3} = 2\,\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
sub_question_2.step_10: From the kinematic equation, we know: $v_{3}^{2}-v_{0}^{2}=2a_{3}s_{3}$
sub_question_2.step_12: Substituting the data yields $v_{0}=1\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, so the conveyor speed should be less than $1\mathrm{m/s}$.

answer: Translate the following text into English. Keep all LaTeX formulas and physical expressions unchanged. Less than $1\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$ Do not add any other content.
image_captions: The image shows two different conveyor belt systems. Figure A depicts a conveyor belt with a solid, dark base, and the belt itself is green. Figure B portrays a conveyor belt with a visible metal frame and legs, again with a green belt. Both belts are straight and appear to be intended for transporting materials horizontally. The backgrounds for both are plain white. The image shows two diagrams, Figure C and Figure D. Figure C depicts a horizontal conveyor belt with a rectangular block on top. The conveyor belt moves to the right with velocity v0. The length of the conveyor is marked as L1. Figure D depicts an inclined conveyor belt with a block on it. The belt moves downwards with velocity v0. The angle of inclination with the horizontal is marked as theta.

step_2.physical_theorem: Kinematic equation for uniformly accelerated motion
